phrases.intent_en: Ask where to find cassoulet ingredients
phrases.text_local: Où puis-je trouver les ingrédients pour faire un cassoulet ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo PWEE-zhuh troo-VAY lay zan-gray-DYAHN poor FAIR un kah-soo-LAY
phrases.cultural_note: Cassoulet is Toulouse's iconic slow-cooked bean and sausage dish; the market sells toulouse sausage, duck confit, and haricot beans — all the key ingredients.

responses.english: At the charcuterie stall, at the back of the market.
responses.phonetic: shay luh shar-koo-TYAY oh FOHN doo mar-SHAY
responses.french: Chez le charcutier, au fond du marché.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a link of Toulouse sausage
phrases.text_local: Je voudrais une saucisse de Toulouse, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh voo-DRAY yoon soh-SEES duh too-LOOZ seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: The famous coarse-ground pork saucisse de Toulouse is an IGP-protected product used in cassoulet and grilled on its own.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the upstairs restaurant serves cassoulet
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que le restaurant à l'étage sert du cassoulet ?
phrases.pronunciation: es-kuh luh res-toh-RAHN ah lay-TAZH sair doo kah-soo-LAY
phrases.cultural_note: The upper floor of the Marché Victor Hugo has several popular restaurants; the market itself closes around 1 pm but the restaurants continue into the afternoon.

responses.english: Yes, go up to the first floor.

phrases.intent_en: Ask the cheese stall for a slice of aged Cantal
phrases.text_local: Je voudrais une tranche de Cantal vieux, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh voo-DRAY yoon TRAHNSH duh kahn-TAL VYUH seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Marché Victor Hugo has excellent fromageries stocking cheeses from the Occitanie region and beyond.

phrases.intent_en: Ask what time the market closes
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure ferme le marché ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel ur FAIRM luh mar-SHAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Marché Victor Hugo is open Tuesday to Sunday mornings and typically closes around 1:30 pm.
